## [06:07 - 09:10]

But the problem with this is that there is a risk with doing tips content. And that is that people can't see when they get done with the tips webinar that you've only given them small pieces of the puzzle. Frankly, this is the easiest webinar to create, but usually has the lowest conversion rate. Because they feel like they can do what it takes to get the transformation without actually buying your product, because you just showed them three cool things that maybe they didn't know or didn't know how to do to be able to achieve the big transformation. And so then they it's like something like there's already so much to do here. I'll do this and see what happens and I'll buy later. So I actually don't like doing this webinar very much to put it bluntly. It's not my favorite webinar structure anymore. And I caution you to make sure that you carefully choose whatever tips if you do do this webinar that you give them so that you give them something that they need, but you don't satisfy the full transformation. And for every bite that you teach, every one of these little tips, you need to point out what they still don't know so that they recognize that they really do need to buy your product to get everything. And be aware also that tips content tends to be less effective for higher end sales. So it's if it's going to be effective, it tends to be effective in the under five hundred dollar products is where it is most effective. ## [15:18 - 16:04]

which most people think is the most important part of the webinar is actually clearly not first. The most important part of the webinar is your close.
